behavior_summary: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea is an enter-fast-area trigger handler that runs when an actor enters a region; it scans nearby items and performs two main gameplay actions: a cleanup pass that removes specific unwanted items on the same coarse tile, and an egg-swap/respawn pass that matches egg-like items and spawns replacement items.

Cleanup pass: iterate items in the provided area/list, compare coarse tile positions (X/Y masked with 0x7E00), skip items already marked with status bit 0x0080, and destroy items whose type equals 0x49 or 2.

Egg-swap/respawn pass: find egg-candidate items (filtered by Item::getUnkEggType compared to a constant that depends on global flag postEarthQuake), compute egg identifiers via Egg::getEggId, match pairs by bitwise AND of egg IDs, create a replacement Item::create preserving type and frame, Item::pop it at a computed (x,y) and at z+1, and mark the spawned item by ORing status bit 0x0080 so it won't be reprocessed.
notable_control_flow: Handler sets iteration context (engine 'set info') and uses 'process exclude' to avoid processing the triggering actor/item.
Two distinct passes over area items: first a destruction/cleanup loop, then a nested search/compare loop to find egg matches and spawn replacements.
Branch on global flag postEarthQuake selects which egg-type constant is used for egg-candidate filtering (observed constants 0x2F vs 0x30).
Per-match behavior uses a found_flag to avoid duplicate spawns and only proceeds to create/pop a new item when a matching pair is identified.
open_questions: What exact gameplay meaning do the type constants 0x49 and 2 have (specific item identities)?
What concrete semantics does status bit 0x0080 carry beyond 'marked to skip' (e.g., temporary spawn marker, persistent flag)?
What does Item::getUnkEggType return in domain terms (egg family, breed, or UI subtype) and what do the constants 0x2F / 0x30 represent?
Is the parameter at [BP+06h] strictly an item-list pointer, an area descriptor, or a combined iteration context (engine-specific)?
Are there any additional side effects (messages, animations) tied to the create/pop beyond the status bit OR that are not visible in the disassembly?

main_function.name: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: When an actor enters the fast-area trigger, tidy up unwanted items on the same tile and detect/match egg-like items to respawn or swap matching items by creating and popping replacement items while marking them to avoid reprocessing.
entry_conditions: Triggered by an U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea event when an actor enters the region associated with this handler.
Requires an area/item-list pointer passed in (used as the iteration base).

function_map.name: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area enter trigger that performs cleanup and egg-swap/respawn logic

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Read item X coordinate for coarse/fine position comparisons and spawn X computation

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Read item Y coordinate for coarse/fine position comparisons and spawn Y computation

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Read item Z/layer used to compute spawn layer (commonly source Z + 1)

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Return status flags (handler tests bit 0x0080 to skip already-marked items)

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Return item type id; used to identify destructible types and to pass to Item::create

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Return item's frame/index to preserve visual appearance when creating replacements

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Destroy/remove items found in cleanup pass

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: Create a new item instance by type/frame to be later popped into the world

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Place/pop the created item at computed (x,y,z) coordinates

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: OR status bit(s) (notably 0x0080) into an item to mark it

function_map.name: Item::getUnkEggType
function_map.role: Return an egg-related subtype used to filter egg candidates (compared to constants conditioned on postEarthQuake)

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Return an egg identifier used for matching two eggs by bitwise AND

function_map.name: U8P_GLOBSWAP::set info
function_map.role: Engine/usecode operation to set up iteration/processing context for the fast-area scan

function_map.name: U8P_GLOBSWAP::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine/usecode operation to exclude the triggering actor/item from iteration

external_calls.purpose: Establish iteration/handler context for scanning area items before loops begin
Exclude the fast-area trigger's actor/item from being scanned/processed
Compute coarse (masked) and spawn X coordinates for comparisons and Item::pop
Compute coarse (masked) and spawn Y coordinates for comparisons and Item::pop
Read source Z to determine spawn Z (typically source Z + 1)
Check status flags and skip items already marked (test 0x0080)
Identify items to destroy (types 0x49 and 2) and to provide type to Item::create
Remove items selected by the cleanup pass
Filter egg candidates by comparing returned subtype to a constant (branch depends on postEarthQuake flag)
Retrieve egg identifier used to test matching pairs (via bitwise AND)
Preserve visual frame when creating replacement items
Create replacement item instance of matched type/frame before popping
Place the created item in world at computed (x,y,z) coordinates
Set status bit 0x0080 on spawned/created item to mark it (prevent reprocessing)

reads.name: postEarthQuake
reads.likely_effect: Select which egg-type constant is used for egg candidate filtering (observed constants 0x2F when 0, 0x30 when 1), altering which eggs are matched/spawned

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea is an enter-fast-area trigger handler that runs when an actor enters a region; it scans nearby items and performs two main gameplay actions: a cleanup pass that removes specific unwanted items on the same coarse tile, and an egg-swap/respawn pass that matches egg-like items and spawns replacement items.
